what_it_does: Implements the agent-facing MCP server (C7) for the Company system: it exposes generic, node-type-agnostic tools over a shared Suite via FastMCP, uses a lazy Suite proxy to avoid constructing a second Suite at import, defines a registry-native safe/operator posture tag, discovers modular tools from mcp_face/tools, and provides the cognition-engine face (inspect, run, reduce, cascade, role authoring, capture, relations, marks).
observations: A _LazySuite proxy defers real Suite construction until first attribute access or an explicit build_mcp(suite) bind.
build_mcp(suite) lets a caller inject its own Suite and raises RuntimeError on a conflicting rebind.
SAFE = ToolAnnotations(posture='safe') marks read-only/low-harm tools for the non-operator tier.
Modular tools are loaded via pkgutil from the mcp_face.tools package, so adding a resource file adds tools without editing this file.
Most tools are thin wrappers that delegate to Suite methods or runtime.cognition engine functions.
The cognition face covers inspect, configure, run, reduce, cascade, role authoring, capture, find_relations and mark verbs.
_fire_role_and_persist centralizes turn-id minting, input resolution, role firing, run:// persistence and op.run record emission.
capabilities() has special inline handling for 'chains' and 'features' sections.
The file contains extensive inline design comments that act as a ledger of decisions and floors.
standouts: The _LazySuite proxy solves the second-Suite-at-import problem while keeping tool registration at import time.
The registry-native SAFE posture annotation encodes tiered access inline on each tool definition.
A single shared fire-and-persist path serves both run_role and run_draft.
File-discovered modular tool registration keeps the server extensible without central edits.
conventions: Heavy inline documentation records design rationale, gap fixes and policy floors.
Tool wrappers reuse Suite/engine functions rather than reimplementing behavior.
Generic verbs over node types are preferred; node operations are consolidated in mcp_face/tools/node.py.
Literal types are used for enum-like parameter constraints.
The module mutates sys.path at import time to reach company packages.
Global module-level objects (mcp, SUITE) are used for registration and proxy binding.
concerns: sys.path mutation at import time can affect the Python environment unexpectedly.
The file is large and mixes MCP server wiring, cognition face, helpers and policy comments.
run_role and _fire_role_and_persist carry many positional/keyword parameters.
_LazySuite relies on a global mutable class variable _real for Suite binding.
Tool docstrings embed UI guidance and policy notes, increasing surface area.
notes: This is the shared agent-facing MCP surface: a thin, import-safe wrapper over the Suite and cognition engine with explicit safe/operator posture tagging.
questions: What exactly does _guard_tools enforce, and when is it invoked?
What tools are registered by each module under mcp_face/tools?
How does the remote gateway read and enforce the posture annotation?
What are the exact semantics of the coordinate and policy parameters in run_role?
How does Suite.ensure_resident load and evict the embedder resource?
What is the full behavior of the mark tool's value and evidence handling?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to expose generic agent verbs over the shared Suite via FastMCP; the file also serves as the full cognition-engine face, which is broader but aligned with the stated shared-layer goal.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the MCP agent face a thin, safe, reusable wrapper over the existing Suite and cognition engine, avoiding duplicate Suite construction and keeping operator-gated governance actions off this face.
novelty: The lazy Suite proxy combined with a bindable factory that lets another face (e.g. the bridge) reuse the same FastMCP tool manager without constructing a second Suite at import.
contribution: A centralized, import-safe MCP tool surface that reuses the shared Suite and cognition engine, with explicit safe/operator posture tagging and modular tool discovery.
summary_for_embedding: company/mcp_face/server.py is the FastMCP agent face that exposes generic graph, cognition and inspection verbs over the shared Suite using a lazy Suite proxy and registry-native posture annotations.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a reusable, import-safe MCP server face that binds to the process's single Suite and exposes read-only safe tools plus operator-gated mutation and cognition verbs.

symbols.does: Lazy proxy that defers Suite construction until first use.
symbols.description: Class-level proxy storing the real Suite in a class variable; __getattr__ forwards attribute access to a lazily built Suite, and __dir__ returns dir(Suite) so tool schemas can be built at import without instantiating the underlying Suite.

symbols.does: Builds the default Suite if none is bound.
symbols.description: Class method that constructs a Suite from FsStore and a discovered NodeRegistry when the proxy has not yet been bound to a real instance.

symbols.does: Forwards missing attribute lookups to the real Suite.
symbols.description: Only called for names not present on the proxy; triggers _ensure and returns the attribute from the underlying Suite, making the proxy transparent at call time.

symbols.does: Returns the attribute list of Suite without instantiating it.
symbols.description: Used at decoration time to enumerate create_* method names; because these are class-level on Suite, dir(Suite) is available without constructing an instance.

symbols.does: Binds a caller-supplied Suite to the proxy and returns the FastMCP server.
symbols.description: Factory that lets the bridge or tests inject its own Suite into SUITE before any tool fires; raises RuntimeError if a different Suite is already bound.

symbols.does: Returns the full node-type library projection.
symbols.description: Read-only MCP tool annotated SAFE; delegates to SUITE.object_info for rendering and composition metadata.

symbols.does: Queries node-types that produce a given port type.
symbols.description: Read-only SAFE tool; returns SUITE.list_by_type(output_type).

symbols.does: Lists all graphs in the substrate.
symbols.description: Read-only SAFE tool; returns SUITE.list_graphs().

symbols.does: Wires two node ports in a graph.
symbols.description: Operator-only mutation tool; calls SUITE.connect with graph, source and target node/port names and returns 'ok'.

symbols.does: Updates a node's config in a graph.
symbols.description: Operator-only mutation tool; passes a copy of the config dict to SUITE.set_config.

symbols.does: Executes a graph and returns run/cached/stuck summary.
symbols.description: Operator-only tool; calls SUITE.run and returns sorted ran/cached lists plus stuck information.

symbols.does: Returns current state of a graph.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.state for nodes, statuses, outputs and addresses.

symbols.does: Returns each node's output in a graph.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.results.

symbols.does: Lists or drills into the surfaced-action approval inbox.
symbols.description: Supports filtering by sid, status, unresolved_only, cap and detail level; returns concise rows or a single full item.

symbols.does: Returns the self-modification audit ledger.
symbols.description: Operator tool; returns newest-first commit history with sha, subject, timestamp, changed files and revert flag.

symbols.does: Returns recent captured trajectory events.
symbols.description: Operator tool; returns newest-first actions via SUITE.events(limit).

symbols.does: Returns the now-view and presence snapshot for a graph.
symbols.description: Defaults graph to 'codebase' when empty; delegates to SUITE.now.

symbols.does: Converses with the right-hand-man about the Company.
symbols.description: Operator tool; returns SUITE.chat(message, graph), which answers from live ground truth without skipping approval gates.

symbols.does: Returns the chief-of-staff inbox triaged into lanes.
symbols.description: Operator tool; returns SUITE.inbox_lanes().

symbols.does: Returns the source-of-truth registry projection.
symbols.description: Supports scoped section queries including special 'chains' and 'features' sections; returns a concise overview when called with no args.

symbols.does: Builds the chains section payload.
symbols.description: Nested helper that lists registered flows and saved cascades with their invocation verbs.

symbols.does: Builds the features section payload.
symbols.description: Nested helper that reads design/register.json and returns the closed feature inventory id-set.

symbols.does: Adapts cognition engine emits onto the Suite event log.
symbols.description: Helper that forwards (kind, payload) to SUITE._emit with a summary and flattened payload fields.

symbols.does: Resolves a registered role id or draft dict to a Role instance.
symbols.description: Uses runtime.authoring helpers to validate ids and render/load draft role sources from a temp module.

symbols.does: Inspects the cognition registries.
symbols.description: Read-only SAFE tool; supports role drill-down with authoring hints, section queries, and concise/detailed overview.

symbols.does: Builds an authorable output-field row from a Pydantic model field.
symbols.description: Nested helper that converts Literal to enum and primitive types to registry type names for round-trip authoring.

symbols.does: Looks up models matching a role's requirements.
symbols.description: Read-only SAFE tool; resolves role requirements or direct capability strings via SUITE.models_for_role.

symbols.does: Returns available input addresses and capability model support.
symbols.description: Read-only SAFE tool; delegates to SUITE.available_inputs with optional role or model projection.

symbols.does: Returns the closed output_schema field-type registry.
symbols.description: Read-only SAFE tool; delegates to SUITE.field_types.

symbols.does: Lists skill://, context:// and guide:// registries.
symbols.description: Read-only SAFE tool; returns ids, labels, descriptions and staleness for guides.

symbols.does: Formats a registry into id/label/description rows.
symbols.description: Nested helper used for skills and contexts; reads entries from the supplied registry dict.

symbols.does: Resolves, attaches a page face, or lists page faces for an address.
symbols.description: Supports op='read' (default), 'attach_page', and 'pages'; reuses runtime.cognition.resolve_address and runtime.page_face helpers.

symbols.does: Configures and runs a registered role, persisting the output.
symbols.description: Resolves the role, resolves address-valued inputs, fires via runtime.cognition.run_role, persists to run:// address, emits an op.run record, and returns output metadata.

symbols.does: Shared fire-and-persist tail for run_role and run_draft.
symbols.description: Mints turn id, resolves inputs, calls run_role, stores output, emits telemetry, and handles model base_url routing and policy/think options.

symbols.does: Runs a one-off draft role without writing it to the registry.
symbols.description: Resolves a draft role dict, then reuses _fire_role_and_persist; the temp module is removed after loading.

symbols.does: Runs a role over a batch of items.
symbols.description: Resolves the role and delegates to _run_items_and_shape for per-item execution and shaped results.

symbols.does: Executes a role across items and shapes the response.
symbols.description: Shared helper for run_items and run_draft_items; runs each item through the role and returns aggregated outputs.

symbols.does: Runs a draft role over a batch of items.
symbols.description: Resolves a draft role dict and reuses _run_items_and_shape.

symbols.does: Reduces a list of run:// addresses by role, rule, or cluster.
symbols.description: Delegates to runtime.cognition.run_reduce with the selected mode and parameters.

symbols.does: Lists available reduce rule names.
symbols.description: Returns the rule registry ids via runtime.cognition.reduce_rule_names.

symbols.does: Saves a cascade declaration.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.save_cascade.

symbols.does: Lists saved cascades.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.list_cascades.

symbols.does: Runs a saved cascade with optional inputs.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.run_cascade.

symbols.does: Previews a turn rendering.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.preview_turn.

symbols.does: Proposes a new role via the surfaced approval gate.
symbols.description: Operator tool; surfaces a role spec for operator approval rather than writing directly.

symbols.does: Proposes an edit to an existing role.
symbols.description: Operator tool; surfaces the updated role spec for operator approval.

symbols.does: Proposes deletion of a role.
symbols.description: Operator tool; surfaces a role deletion for operator approval.

symbols.does: Captures corpus units under a project/session.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.capture_corpus with role, units, project, session and other parameters.

symbols.does: Finds semantic relations across near and far embedding spaces.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.find_relations.

symbols.does: Applies a typed mark to a target with optional evidence.
symbols.description: Operator tool; delegates to SUITE.mark with value, confidence, source pass and evidence.

symbols.does: Installs guards around tool invocations.
symbols.description: Top-level helper that appears to wrap the tool manager's callables; exact guard semantics are not fully shown in the truncated file.

symbols.does: Creates a guarded wrapper for a tool function.
symbols.description: Nested factory inside _guard_tools that returns a _guarded callable preserving the original signature.

symbols.does: The actual guarded invocation wrapper.
symbols.description: Innermost function that receives positional and keyword args and presumably enforces a policy before calling the original tool.
